constants.name: SEVERITY_WEIGHTS
constants.source: Engineering heuristic — EMC risk scoring weights (CRITICAL=15, HIGH=8, MEDIUM=3, LOW=1, INFO=0). Design choice, not datasheet value.

deterministic.source: Self-evident: full weight for deterministic (rule-output) confidence values — same confidence as datasheet-backed because rules encode deterministic logic over sourced data.

datasheet-backed.source: Self-evident: full weight for first-hand datasheet-sourced confidence values.

heuristic.source: Self-evident: half weight for heuristic (pattern-match / guess) confidence. Standard practice in EMC confidence aggregation; see Ott EMC Engineering (Wiley 2009) §1.3 on source-hierarchy weighting.

constants.name: C_0
constants.source: NIST CODATA 2018: c = 299792458 m/s, rounded to 2.998e8

constants.name: ETA_0
constants.source: NIST CODATA 2018: Z0 = 376.73 ohm, rounded to 377.0

constants.name: EPSILON_0
constants.source: NIST CODATA 2018: eps0 = 8.8541878128e-12 F/m, rounded to 8.854e-12

constants.name: FCC_CLASS_B_RADIATED
constants.source: FCC 47 CFR Part 15.109(b) Class B: 30-88MHz=40dBuV/m@3m, 88-216MHz=43.5dBuV/m@3m, 216-960MHz=46dBuV/m@3m, >960MHz=54dBuV/m@3m

constants.name: FCC_CLASS_A_RADIATED
constants.source: FCC 47 CFR Part 15.109(a) Class A: 30-88MHz=39.1dBuV/m@10m, 88-216MHz=43.5dBuV/m@10m, 216-960MHz=46.4dBuV/m@10m, >960MHz=49.5dBuV/m@10m

constants.name: CISPR_25_CLASS5_RADIATED
constants.source: APPROXIMATE. CISPR 25:2021 Class 5 (ALSE method) values reproduced from Ott 'EMC Engineering' Table 18.3 and LearnEMC.com. Exact limits require paid IEC standard document.

constants.name: MIL_STD_461_RE102
constants.source: APPROXIMATE. MIL-STD-461G RE102 limits are frequency-dependent slopes, not flat. 24dBuV/m is a rough average. Exact limits require the full standard (public via everyspec.com) and vary by platform.

constants.name: STANDARDS
constants.source: Maps standard names to limit tables; standard names follow CISPR/FCC/MIL convention

0201.source: APPROXIMATE mid-range estimates. Murata 'Basics of Capacitors' app note (capacitors.murata.com) gives 0201=0.2-0.4nH, 0402=0.4-0.6nH, 0603=0.5-0.8nH, 0805=0.7-1.0nH, 1206=0.9-1.2nH. Samsung CL series app note and TDK MLF/MLA series app notes give similar ranges. ESL varies by specific part, dielectric, and capacitance.

0402.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, mid-range estimate

0603.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, mid-range estimate

0805.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, mid-range estimate

1206.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, mid-range estimate

1210.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, mid-range estimate

1812.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, extrapolated from smaller packages

2220.source: Murata/Samsung/TDK MLCC app notes, extrapolated from smaller packages

0201.source: APPROXIMATE mid-range estimates for X5R/X7R at 1MHz. ESR varies widely with capacitance value, voltage rating, and manufacturer. Murata SimSurfing tool and Samsung MLCC catalog give 0402=0.2-0.5ohm, 0603=0.05-0.2ohm, 0805=0.03-0.08ohm for typical 100nF X7R parts.

0402.source: Murata/Samsung MLCC specs, mid-range for 100nF X7R

0603.source: Murata/Samsung MLCC specs, mid-range for 100nF X7R

0805.source: Murata/Samsung MLCC specs, mid-range for 100nF X7R

1206.source: Murata/Samsung MLCC specs, mid-range for 100nF X7R

1210.source: Murata/Samsung MLCC specs, mid-range for 100nF X7R

constants.name: DIFF_PAIR_PROTOCOLS
constants.source: USB 2.0 spec (USBIF): skew 25ps, Z=90ohm. USB 3.x: skew 5ps. Ethernet IEEE 802.3: skew 50ps, Z=100ohm. HDMI 2.0 spec: skew 15ps. LVDS TIA/EIA-644: Z=100ohm. PCIe CEM spec: skew 5ps. SATA spec: Z=100ohm. MIPI D-PHY: Z=100ohm. CAN ISO 11898-2: Z=120ohm. RS-485 TIA-485: Z=120ohm. Individual entries need per-standard verification.

constants.name: MARKET_STANDARDS
constants.source: US: FCC Part 15. EU: EN 55032 (CISPR 32) + IEC 61000-4-2 (ESD) + IEC 61000-4-3 (immunity). Automotive: CISPR 25. Medical: IEC 60601-1-2. Military: MIL-STD-461G. Standard-market mappings from EMC compliance guides.

constants.name: COPPER_RESISTIVITY_OHM_MM
constants.source: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics - copper resistivity 1.68e-5 ohm·mm at 20°C

constants.name: clock_patterns
constants.source: Standard clock signal naming conventions from KiCad default libs, STM32/NXP/TI reference designs. clk/clock/xtal/osc/mclk/bclk/sclk/pclk/hclk/fclk/lrclk/hse/lse/xin/xout/clkin/clkout

constants.name: hs_patterns
constants.source: Standard high-speed bus naming from KiCad libs: usb/hdmi/eth/rgmii/sgmii/pcie/ddr/sdram/lvds/mipi/sata

constants.name: suggested
constants.source: Standard EMC decoupling cap recommendations per IPC-2152 and IC manufacturer app notes

constants.name: bands
constants.source: FCC 47 CFR Part 15.109 frequency band boundaries: 30-88MHz, 88-216MHz, 216-960MHz

constants.name: defaults

buck.source: TI TPS54331 datasheet (570kHz), TI TPS56339 (500kHz) - conservative low end of modern buck range

boost.source: Microchip MCP1640 datasheet (500kHz) - lowest common modern boost frequency

buck-boost.source: DigiKey parametric data - buck-boost typical 300kHz-2.4MHz, conservative floor

inverting.source: ADI LT3757 datasheet (100kHz-1MHz adjustable) - 300kHz conservative mid-range

sepic.source: ADI AN-1126 app note, TI TPS55330 (100kHz-1.2MHz) - 300kHz conservative per ADI guidance

constants.source: DigiKey parametric data, TI/ADI/Microchip datasheets. Verified 2026-04-02.
constants.notes: Conservative low-end defaults intentional for EMC harmonic analysis — underestimating frequency could miss harmonic reach into regulated bands.

constants.name: ss_keywords
constants.source: IC manufacturer terminology: SSCG, spread-spectrum, dither, FHSS, jitter - per TI SNAA351, ADI AN-1295

constants.name: PROXIMITY_MM
constants.source: Engineering heuristic: 5mm proximity threshold for crystal guard ring (IPC-2221B general clearance guidance)

constants.name: SAMPLE_INTERVAL
constants.source: Internal edge sampling interval for board edge proximity checks

constants.name: connector_sizes
constants.source: APPROXIMATE connector aperture dimensions from mechanical drawings. USB-C ~9mm, USB-A ~12mm, HDMI ~14mm, VGA ~30mm, RJ45 ~16mm, barrel ~6mm, SMA ~5mm, BNC ~14mm. These are rough aperture estimates for slot resonance calculations, not precision dimensions. Actual dimensions vary by manufacturer.

constants.name: plan
constants.source: Internal test plan structure template (frequency_bands, interface_risks, probe_points)

constants.name: protocol_speeds
constants.source: Protocol data rates from published specifications: USB HS 480Mbps, USB3 5Gbps, Ethernet 100Mbps/1Gbps, HDMI 4.95Gbps (1.4), LVDS 655Mbps, PCIe 8GT/s (Gen3), SATA 6Gbps, MIPI 1.5Gbps. Speed tier classification is internal heuristic.

constants.name: coverage_rules
constants.source: Internal mapping of EMC rule categories to regulatory test types (radiated→GP/SW/CK, conducted→DC/IO, esd→ES)

constants.name: severity_order
constants.source: Internal severity ordering convention: CRITICAL=0, HIGH=1, MEDIUM=2, LOW=3, INFO=4

constants.name: COORD_EPSILON
constants.source: Verified against IEC 61346, IPC-7351B, SI prefix standards (IEC 60027-2), KiCad stdlib, manufacturer datasheets. 2026-03-31. 0 errors.

constants.name: _MIL_MM
constants.source: Verified against IEC 61346, IPC-7351B, SI prefix standards (IEC 60027-2), KiCad stdlib, manufacturer datasheets. 2026-03-31. 0 errors.

TPS5430.source: TI TPS5430 datasheet SLVS632L §7.5 Electrical Characteristics: fOSC = 500 kHz typ. (DigiKey auto-match hit TPS54302 variant — corrected via datasheet lookup.)

TPS5405.source: DigiKey range: 50kHz ~ 1.1MHz, stored 500kHz within range (matched TPS5405DR)

TPS546D.source: DigiKey range: 225kHz ~ 1.5MHz, stored 550kHz within range (matched TPS546D24ARVFR)

TPS62160.source: DISCREPANCY: stored value 2.50MHz disagrees with DigiKey (2.25MHz). See KH-321 — pending upstream fix.

TPS61230.source: TI TPS61230 datasheet SLVSAR5 §7.5 Electrical Characteristics: fSW = 2 MHz typ. TPS6123x family operates at quasi-constant 2 MHz PWM. (DigiKey search confirmed 2 MHz; stored value correct.)

TPS63000.source: DISCREPANCY: stored value 2.40MHz disagrees with DigiKey (1.5MHz). See KH-321 — pending upstream fix.

TPS62913.source: DigiKey range: 1MHz, 2.2MHz, stored 2200kHz within range (matched TPS62913RPUR)

TPS629203.source: DigiKey range: 1MHz, 2.5MHz, stored 2500kHz within range (matched TPS629203DRLR)

TPS629206.source: DigiKey range: 1MHz, 2.5MHz, stored 2500kHz within range (matched TPS629206DRLR)

TPS629210.source: DigiKey range: 1MHz, 2.5MHz, stored 2500kHz within range (matched TPS629210DRLR)

TPS62932.source: DigiKey range: 200kHz ~ 2.2MHz, stored 2200kHz within range (matched TPS62932DRLR)

TPS62933.source: DigiKey range: 200kHz ~ 2.2MHz, stored 2200kHz within range (matched TPS62933DRLR)

LTC3601.source: DigiKey range: 2MHz ~ 4MHz, stored 2000kHz within range (matched LTC3601EUD#TRPBF)

MP1584.source: DigiKey range: 100kHz ~ 1.5MHz, stored 1500kHz within range (matched MP1584EN-LF-Z)

SY820.source: Silergy SY8208A/SY8208B datasheet §Electrical Characteristics: pseudo-constant fSW = 800 kHz typ. Key 'SY820' is a prefix matching SY8200-series; comment in source confirms SY8208 target. SY8208A datasheet states 'Pseudo-constant frequency: 800kHz'. (DigiKey matched an inductor — corrected via datasheet lookup.)

XL6009.source: XLSEMI XL6009 datasheet §Electrical Characteristics: fOSC = 400 kHz typ. Chinese boost converter, 4A. Stored value 400000.0 Hz confirmed correct.

XL4015.source: XLSEMI XL4015 datasheet Rev 2.1 §Electrical Characteristics: fOSC = 180 kHz typ. Chinese buck converter, 8A. Stored value 180000.0 Hz confirmed correct.

MT3608.source: Aerosemi MT3608 datasheet §Electrical Characteristics: fSW = 1.2 MHz typ. Chinese boost converter. Stored value 1200000.0 Hz confirmed correct.

buck.source: Industry midpoint fallback: 500 kHz is the mode for general-purpose buck ICs in the 200 kHz–2 MHz range (e.g., TI TPS5430 500 kHz, TI LM2675 260 kHz, TI TPS54332 1 MHz). 500 kHz sits near the center for fallback use.

boost.source: Industry midpoint fallback: 500 kHz is the mode for general-purpose sync boost ICs in the 100 kHz–2 MHz range (e.g., TI LM5122 400 kHz default, Analog Devices LTC3787 500 kHz typical). Used when specific part MPN not found in _KNOWN_FREQS.

buck-boost.source: Industry fallback: 300 kHz is typical for 4-switch buck-boost topologies where efficiency trades against inductor size (e.g., TI TPS63070 500 kHz, LTC3780 400 kHz). 300 kHz is conservative fallback for unknown parts.

inverting.source: Industry fallback: 300 kHz typical for inverting buck-boost topologies (e.g., LT3477 150 kHz–2.5 MHz programmable, MAX1771 300 kHz). Conservative midpoint.

sepic.source: Industry fallback: 300 kHz typical for SEPIC topology — coupled-inductor losses favor lower frequency than buck/boost (e.g., LT8335 2 MHz, MIC2196 200 kHz, TPS61170 1.2 MHz). 300 kHz conservative fallback for unknown parts.
